Frequency Timing

Best Time for Window Cleaning in the Shuswap: A Season-by-Season Guide

Published 2026-05-13 · Wagon Windows

The Shuswap has four distinct seasons that each leave a specific mark on residential glass. Knowing when each accumulation phase peaks — and when to clean relative to it — is the difference between windows that look good for three months and windows that stay clear most of the year.

Need professional window cleaning in the Shuswap?

Get an Instant Estimate (403) 837-5092

Spring: Late April to Early June, Post-Cottonwood Peak

Spring is the single most important window cleaning appointment of the year for Shuswap homeowners, and it's the one that fills earliest. Cottonwood season in Salmon Arm typically begins in the third or fourth week of April and peaks through May, with seed release continuing into early June depending on the year's temperature pattern. During peak release, cottonwood fluff coats outdoor surfaces visibly — windows, screens, gutters, and outdoor furniture all receive a dense coating of white organic matter that tracks inside on shoes and clothing. The right time to clean is after the cottonwood peak has passed but before the summer dust season begins — generally late May to early June. Cleaning during cottonwood release is counterproductive; the windows will be recoated within days. Cleaning before cottonwood releases means your clean windows are the surface the pollen lands on. Post-cottonwood cleaning should include screens without exception. Screen mesh traps cottonwood fluff in a way that glass doesn't — the loose fluff on glass blows off in rain, but what's packed into the mesh is there until it's brushed out. Cottonwood-loaded screens block airflow and harbour fine pollen that continues to shed indoors on every breeze. For Salmon Arm homeowners, calling in March to book a late May or early June appointment is the right timeline. By the time you're looking at cottony windows in mid-May and deciding to call, you're likely booking in late June — after the useful window has closed. Spring is also the right time for gutter cleaning, ideally combined with the window clean. Gutters accumulate cottonwood fluff and seed pods in the same period; a combined window and gutter clean in late May addresses both in one visit.

Early Summer: July, Before Hosting Season Peaks

Not every Shuswap homeowner books a summer clean, but for properties on or near the lake — and for anyone who hosts regularly in July and August — it's a genuine improvement over waiting until fall. July is the optimal timing for a midsummer clean. By early July, the spring pollen load has been cleaned (assuming a May/June clean happened), and the window is open before summer's peak dust, road grime, and activity load accumulates. Lake-facing windows that have been picking up mineral spray through June benefit from a July treatment before the summer's second half of spray exposure adds to the first half's deposits. For vacation properties and Airbnb rentals on the Shuswap Lake shoreline, a July clean just before the peak rental period is the most commercially logical timing. Guests notice window cleanliness in a lake-view property in a way they don't in a suburban home — the view is the product, and spotty glass compromises it. For Salmon Arm inland homes without lake exposure, July is optional rather than essential. The spring clean will still be doing its job well into summer, and the fall clean addresses the late-summer accumulation effectively. The homeowners who most benefit from a July clean are those with significant lakefront exposure, those who entertain extensively in July and August, and those with south or west-facing windows in dusty rural locations near Chase, Enderby, or Armstrong.

  • Lake-view and vacation rentals: July clean is near-essential
  • Homes near agricultural dust (Chase, Enderby, Armstrong): July clean worthwhile
  • Inland Salmon Arm homes with no lake exposure: July optional, spring+fall sufficient

Fall: September to Early October, The Pre-Freeze Window

The fall window cleaning appointment has a hard deadline in the Shuswap: exterior cleaning requires temperatures consistently above 5°C, and Salmon Arm can see overnight frosts from mid-October, with reliable daytime freezing typically arriving in November. The target window for fall cleaning is September 15 through October 15. This timing accomplishes several things simultaneously: it removes the summer accumulation of dust, organic matter, and mineral deposits before they're locked under winter weather; it catches the post-leaf-fall surface film that deposits on glass from deciduous tree activity in September; and it gives a clean starting surface for the low sun angles of winter, which makes any glass imperfection glaringly visible. Fall timing relative to leaf drop requires some judgement. Cleaning before most deciduous leaves have fallen makes the glass available for another surface film. Waiting until all leaves have dropped risks running into cold temperatures or running out of dry weather windows. In Salmon Arm, cleaning in the last week of September through the first week of October typically hits the sweet spot: most leaf drop is complete, and reliable freezing is still weeks away. Fall is also the right time for gutter cleaning, ideally 2–4 weeks after the main leaf drop. A combined fall window and gutter clean — with the gutter portion happening after peak leaf fall — is the most efficient scheduling for homeowners who want to address both before winter.

  • Target window: September 15 – October 15
  • Timing relative to leaf drop: late September to early October is the sweet spot
  • Combine with gutter cleaning for full pre-winter exterior maintenance

Lake Properties vs Inland Properties: Timing Differences

Lake-adjacent properties in Blind Bay, Sorrento, Sicamous, and along the Shuswap Lake shoreline east of Salmon Arm follow a slightly different optimal timing than inland Salmon Arm properties. For lake properties, the spring clean timing shifts slightly earlier — late April or early May rather than late May — for one specific reason: ice-off. Shuswap Lake's ice clears in late March to April most years, and the transition from ice cover to open water creates a period of increased mineral spray activity as wind-driven wave action resumes. Windows on lake-facing properties that have been closed for winter and then exposed to early spring spray accumulate mineral deposits quickly from April onward. A late April clean on a lake property takes advantage of the clean-glass window before significant spray accumulation begins, and a May clean after cottonwood gives a second pass. For winter lake properties — those occupied year-round rather than seasonally — early spring cleaning can also remove winter condensation deposits and salt haze that builds on southern-exposed glass through the short-day period. Inland Salmon Arm properties are less sensitive to the ice-off dynamic and the late May timing is optimal for the spring clean. The main variable is wind exposure: homes in exposed positions in Canoe, South Broadview, or the western hillside neighbourhoods of Salmon Arm face more dust in summer than sheltered properties in established tree-lined streets.

  • Lake properties (Blind Bay, Sorrento, Sicamous): spring clean in late April–early May
  • Inland Salmon Arm: spring clean in late May–early June
  • Both: fall clean September–October before freeze

Booking Lead Times and Why Spring Fills Fastest

Understanding when to clean is only half the equation — the other half is securing the appointment before the date you want is gone. Spring is constrained by both high demand and a fixed window. Everyone who wants a post-cottonwood clean wants it in roughly the same 3–4 week period (late May to mid-June). Add the fact that homeowners tend to call reactively — when the windows look dirty — rather than proactively, and you get a situation where every available May and June appointment is spoken for by mid-May. The homeowners who consistently get their preferred spring date book in March or early April. That's not a long lead time — 6–10 weeks — but it's enough to secure the right slot before demand concentrates. Summer and fall have longer booking windows. July appointments are typically available 2–4 weeks out. Fall appointments through September and October can usually be secured with 1–3 weeks notice, though October dates for the pre-freeze rush get competitive in late September. Wagon Windows accepts bookings through our booking page at wagonwindow.com or by phone at (403) 837-5092. For customers who want to lock in all three annual cleans at once, the same booking process handles it — and it eliminates the annual March reminder-to-call-before-May-fills.

  • Spring: book in March–April for late May–June date
  • Summer: 2–4 weeks lead time typically sufficient
  • Fall: 1–3 weeks most of September; October dates go fast in late Sept

Frequently Asked Questions

What's the absolute best time of year to clean windows in Salmon Arm?

Late May to early June — after cottonwood pollen peaks and before summer dust accumulates. This single clean has the most visible impact of any annual appointment. If you're only doing one clean a year, this is the one.

Can windows be cleaned in winter in Salmon Arm?

Exterior cleaning requires temperatures above 5°C — below that, cleaning solution streaks and runoff freezes. Reliable daytime above-5°C days are uncommon in Salmon Arm from mid-November through February. Interior cleaning can be done year-round. Exterior service is seasonal: April through October is the reliable window.

Should I clean my windows before or after cottonwood season?

After. Cleaning during cottonwood release means your freshly cleaned glass becomes the surface for the next week of pollen accumulation. Wait until the release has peaked and tailed off — typically early June — then clean. Your windows will stay clean significantly longer than if cleaned during the peak.

How early should I book for spring window cleaning in the Shuswap?

March or early April. Spring is the highest-demand period and the most constrained window. Booking 6–10 weeks ahead secures your preferred date. Calling in May typically means June availability at the earliest.
